steps.title: Trace the passage's structure
steps.content: The passage describes two prior studies (Chan et al. and Zwaan et al.), then says the gap between them — the "relative prevalence" question — "motivated" Green and Extavour's 2012 study. The passage is setting up what Green and Extavour did and why, not making an argument or reporting their findings.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘To situate Green and Extavour's study in the context of their field.’: The passage does exactly this: it shows what was already known, identifies the gap, and explains how that gap led to Green and Extavour's work. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘To suggest that Green and Extavour's conclusions would be more persuasive if they incorporated findings from the studies by Chan and colleagues and Zwaan and colleagues.’: The passage makes no suggestion that Green and Extavour should have done anything differently. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘To summarize Green and Extavour's criticism of the study by Chan and colleagues.’: Green and Extavour are not described as criticizing Chan et al.; the gap is presented as a field-wide open question. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘To argue that Green and Extavour's study revealed a previously unrecognized issue that evolutionary biologists need to address.’: The issue was already recognized; the passage says it was poorly understood before Green and Extavour studied it. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
